message: My 7-year-old asked me last week why we have a 'boring box' under the stairs with flashlights and first-aid stuff. I started explaining emergency preparedness, and her eyes glazed over until I said, 'It's like a superhero starter kit—just in case.' Now she wants to help check it every month and add glow sticks 'for nighttime missions.' 😄 Honestly? Her excitement made me actually *do* the thing I'd been putting off. Sometimes the best motivation is a kid asking 'Can I help?' It turned what felt like a chore into something we do together. Plus, now she's not scared of storms—she's just ready. A little refresher on what actually belongs in that kit didn't hurt either. What's one prep task you've been meaning to tackle that a family member could help with?
